channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work has begun on JDK 24, with five features so far proposed for the release: stream gatherers, a vector API, a class-file API, warnings to prepare developers for future restrictions on the use of JNI (Java Native Interface),
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: NYC Department of Education builds the pipeline for future cybersecurity professionals
item.description: There’s been a lot of talk over the past few years about a shortage of skilled cybersecurity talent, but from where we sit at the NYC DOE Division of Information and Instructional Technology (DIIT), we see budding security pros all around us.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: State Department making sure change is more than just a name
item.description: Kelly Fletcher, the State Department’s CIO, said the newly named Bureau of Diplomatic Technology is aligning cybersecurity and customer experience. The post State Department making sure change is more than just a name first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Voicemeeter app sets off Blue Screen of Death woes — here’s what you need to know
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows 11 24H2 update causes Blue Screen of Death crashes for users running Voicemeeter, prompting a block on updates.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Van Halen's unreleased music
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump is losing on TikTok
item.description: TikTok’s influence has become undeniable in the 2024 presidential election, with both camps leaning full force into the idea that if you can’t beat ’em, join ’em. But that plan hasn’t worked out so well for Republican nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok approved election misinformation ads despite its own ban, says watchdog
item.description: Just weeks before the U.S. presidential election, TikTok approved advertisements that contained election disinformation even though it has a ban on political ads, according to a report published Thursday by the nonprofit Global Witness.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: 7 Android 15 features you can bring to any phone today
item.description: No Android 15? No problem. You can still enjoy some of the fancy new features from Google’s latest Android version — no matter who made your current device or how old it might be.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Direction was the internet's first boy band, and Liam Payne its grounding force
item.description: One Direction member Liam Payne was found dead this week after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, Argentina, at just 31.
item.dc:creator: Maria Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Navy logistics modernization effort ready to take next step
item.description: Jeff Baur, the product director for logistics IT for the Navy’s PEO-MLB, said ensuring the new platform meets the users’ needs is a top priority. The post Navy logistics modernization effort ready to take next step first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Easy ways to make calls when vision is a challenge
item.description: For those with visual challenges, there are a number of voice-dialing apps and features to help make phone calls without having to see numbers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Breaking down government hacks: The rise of the modern kill chain
item.description: This year, expect to see a record number of cyberattacks on government agencies as hackers ramp up their efforts ahead of the 2024 U.S. presidential election. The post Breaking down government hacks: The rise of the modern kill chain first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use Task.WhenEach in .NET 9
item.description: The upcoming release of .NET 9 introduces the Task.WhenEach method, which enables developers to handle asynchronous tasks more elegantly. The Task.WhenEach method allows us to iterate through tasks as they complete, without having to call Task.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A10 Networks tackles AI threats with platform updates
item.description: A10 Networks is updating its platforms in a bid to help enterprises deal with increasing risks, including those that might come from AI-driven attacks. The improvements to the network security vendor’s technology span multiple products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI isn’t really that smart yet, Apple researchers warn
item.description: While we wait for the Age Of Apple Intelligence, it may be worth considering a recent Apple research study that exposes critical weaknesses in existing artificial intelligence models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI-powered scam targets 2.5 billion Gmail users in sophisticated phishing attacks
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about a new AI-powered scam that targets Gmail users and is harder to detect unless you're very cautious.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hacked robo-vacuums chase owners, yell slurs
item.description: Some customers report the tiny tech tools chased them around their homes and barraged them with profanities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World
